label: Current in-demand AI engineering tools/stack
value: LLM orchestration (LangChain/LangGraph), vector DBs (Pinecone/pgvector), agent frameworks, MCP-style tool integration, LLM observability (LangSmith)
notes: This space moves monthly. Always re-verify before committing to a stack for a real project.

label: Current in-demand data/BI stack
value: BigQuery/Snowflake + dbt for transforms + Looker or Tableau for BI, Python/pandas for heavier analysis
notes: dbt is the field's standard transform tool, independent of whether any individual claims it on their own resume.

label: Current in-demand cloud/infra stack
value: Terraform for IaC, Kubernetes for orchestration, AWS/GCP/Azure as the big three, GPU orchestration (Ray, Slurm) rising with AI workloads
notes: GPU cluster management specifically is a fast-growing niche driven by AI training/inference demand.

label: Current in-demand AI hardware & silicon stack
value: HDLs (SystemVerilog/Verilog, VHDL) with UVM verification; ASIC EDA from Synopsys & Cadence (synthesis, place-and-route, timing closure); FPGA toolchains (AMD/Xilinx Vivado, Intel Quartus); Python for verification and accelerator/ML co-design; CUDA and accelerator-architecture skills for AI-chip work
notes: Silicon tooling moves slowly vs the AI-software layer — Verilog/VHDL and the Synopsys/Cadence EDA duopoly are stable for years. The fast-moving edge is AI-accelerator architecture (GPUs/NPUs/TPUs) and hardware-for-ML co-design.

label: Current in-demand ML modeling & computational-science stack
value: PyTorch dominant (JAX rising for research/scale); Hugging Face ecosystem; NumPy/SciPy/pandas + scikit-learn for classical/tabular; experiment tracking (Weights & Biases, MLflow); distributed/GPU training (FSDP, DeepSpeed); the math core stays constant — linear algebra, calculus, probability, optimization
notes: Frameworks drift (Torch vs JAX) but the underlying math and the train/eval/diagnose method don't. Distinct from ai_eng_hot_tools: this is building and training models, not wiring finished LLMs into apps.
